compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Completion-verifier gap remediated (AC5 rollback_recovery): strengthened tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py::test_rollback_recovery_detects_and_repairs_stale_channel_snapshot to exercise the FULL fsck path -- it now (1) asserts fsck._check_snapshot surfaces SNAPSHOT_STALE_CHANNEL for a legacy snapshot missing creation_channel with a retained CREATE, (2) runs fsck --repair-snapshots and asserts the rebuilt SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state carries creation_channel=jira + inferred, and (3) asserts the finding clears after repair. Also preemptively pinned the AC7 docs/concurrency.md downgrade matrix via test_schema_coverage.py::test_concurrency_docs_pin_creation_channel_downgrade_matrix. Both in HEAD (3be8f6bf1). Re-verifying.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Completion-verifier gaps remediated comprehensively (full AC audit): AC1 -- test_recorded_channel_survives_compaction now PARAMETRIZED over all five channels cli/mcp/python/jira/import (each compacted -> SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state carries the exact channel, no marker, SNAPSHOT-only replay returns it). AC2 -- inferred test now also asserts SNAPSHOT-only replay preserves jira+marker. AC3 -- broadened the process_snapshot guard from 'creation_channel not in compiled_state' to also re-evaluate a marker-less provisional 'unknown' (the plan's What: absent/None/provisional-unknown re-evaluated; concrete recorded + already-inferred bypass), plus two new reducer tests (provisional-unknown -> jira on 3-signal, else stays unknown). AC5 fsck detect+repair + AC7 trust-boundary/concurrency pins from prior rounds retained. _processors ceiling 864->867. Re-verifying.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Creation provenance stops being auditable if compaction retires the original CREATE and the replacement SNAPSHOT omits or reinterprets the channel. This story makes recorded, inferred, and unknown provenance durable across normal compaction, conservative-horizon compaction, SNAPSHOT-only replay, legacy snapshots, and snapshot rebuild/recovery.

## What

- Preserve creation_channel and the optional true-only creation_channel_inferred marker inside SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state. Do not add them to the SNAPSHOT envelope, whose author/env_id identify the compactor.
- Confirm src/rebar/_commands/compact.py continues to copy the complete reduced state while stripping only updated_at and the legacy signature mirror. Add a contract test that calls _snapshot_strip_keys() and asserts its result is disjoint from {"creation_channel", "creation_channel_inferred"}. The guard is deliberately test-enforced rather than a production assertion because the strip set is static internal policy and CI is the enforcement boundary.
- Document the rollback schema-state matrix: an existing new-code SNAPSHOT retains unknown compiled_state keys if replayed by old code; a first old-code compaction of raw creation-bearing CREATE events omits the field from that SNAPSHOT but retains the CREATE permanently as .retired; a never-compacted ticket retains the active CREATE value while the old reader hides it. The MVP has no writer-only binary mode. Before a full binary downgrade, operators stop every scheduled or manual rebar compact invocation. If the pause is violated, they record the affected ticket IDs from downgrade-window compaction activity, re-deploy the new binary, and rebuild those snapshots from the full log; include_retired=True replays the retained CREATE and restores creation_channel.
- At the end of process_snapshot, after compiled_state restoration and alongside the existing managed_refs/attestations/keyring migration hooks, call _project_legacy_creation_channel(state). The helper is a no-op for concrete recorded channels or an already-inferred jira value; absent, None, or provisional unknown-without-marker state is re-evaluated from restored ticket_id/author/env_id. A snapshot carrying a recorded channel is restored verbatim, while legacy and partial-migration snapshots receive the deterministic jira/unknown projection.
- Materialize creation_channel_inferred=true into snapshots produced from inferred historical state so later SNAPSHOT-only replay retains the certainty marker without access to retired CREATE files.
- Cover rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log and partial-horizon compaction, including replay of live events after the snapshot.
- Update event/compaction documentation to state that ticket genesis provenance lives in compiled_state and is distinct from SNAPSHOT event authorship. Update docs/output-schemas.md to state that SNAPSHOT-only replay plus snapshot rebuild emit the same creation_channel and creation_channel_inferred values through show/list/search/export as full-log replay.

## Approach Rationale

The compactor already snapshots reducer output and process_snapshot restores every compiled-state key. Preserving the field through that established path avoids a second provenance representation and keeps normal replay and recovery identical. A small legacy migration hook after snapshot restore is necessary because pre-feature snapshots cannot contain the new keys. No event rewrite or snapshot format fork is required. The rollback guidance distinguishes safe carry-forward of keys already present in compiled_state from first-compaction omission. Because .retired sources are retained permanently and rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log reduces with include_retired=True, the omission is recoverable after re-upgrade; a targeted regression proves the compensating-forward path instead of overstating it as irreversible loss.

## Scope

IN: normal and horizon compaction, SNAPSHOT-only replay, legacy SNAPSHOT fallback, snapshot rebuild/recovery from retained .retired CREATEs, field-retention guards, the full-downgrade schema-state/precondition matrix, and compaction documentation.

OUT: changing source_event_uuids semantics, changing SNAPSHOT envelope authorship, rewriting existing snapshots, changing compaction thresholds, or adding a store-format migration.

## Files

- src/rebar/_commands/compact.py: compiled-state retention and full-log rebuild path.
- src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py: detect an old-style SNAPSHOT missing recorded provenance when a retained CREATE carries it and route --repair-snapshots through the existing rebuild seam.
- src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py: post-SNAPSHOT legacy projection using the shared helper.
- tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py: end-to-end retirement and replay.
- tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_snapshot.py: direct recorded/inferred/unknown snapshot projection.
- tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py: rebuild and horizon regression where needed.
- docs/event-schema.md and docs/concurrency.md: SNAPSHOT placement and recovery semantics.
- docs/output-schemas.md: consumer-facing parity between full-log, SNAPSHOT-only, and rebuilt-SNAPSHOT replay.

## Dependencies

Depends on the Jira/import story because the shared field, inference helper, and marker semantics must exist before snapshot migration can preserve them.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Compacting tickets recorded as cli, mcp, python, jira, and import writes the exact channel into SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state, retires the source CREATE, and SNAPSHOT-only replay returns the same value without an inference marker. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k recorded.
- [ ] Compacting an inferred historical Jira ticket materializes creation_channel=jira and creation_channel_inferred=true in compiled_state; SNAPSHOT-only replay preserves both. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k inferred.
- [ ] A pre-feature SNAPSHOT with creation_channel absent, None, or provisional unknown-without-marker projects inferred Jira only for the exact three-signal predicate and otherwise unknown; concrete recorded channels plus already-inferred Jira bypass re-inference. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_snapshot.py -k creation_channel.
- [ ] creation_channel and creation_channel_inferred never appear on the SNAPSHOT envelope and are not removed by _snapshot_strip_keys. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k placement.
- [ ] rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log plus conservative-horizon compaction preserve the same provenance while later live events replay normally. A simulated old-style SNAPSHOT missing creation_channel is detected and repaired from its retained CREATE through the existing fsck --repair-snapshots seam. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k "rebuild or horizon or rollback_recovery" && uv run pytest -q tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py.
- [ ] Existing snapshot consistency, rollback recovery, and forward-compat behavior remain green. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_rollback_recovery.py tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py.
- [ ] As final documentation integrator, verify docs/event-schema.md plus docs/output-schemas.md jointly cover the enum, Jira inference, import semantics, SNAPSHOT placement, actor/environment/source_* distinctions, plus the diagnostic trust boundary. docs/concurrency.md covers rollback states, the compact pause, affected-ID capture, plus full-log rebuild. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_outputs.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py && make lint.

file_impact.reason: preserve provenance in compiled_state and rebuild
new compaction and retirement lifecycle matrix
final integration of full-log, SNAPSHOT, provenance-dimension, and trust-boundary documentation
detect and repair old-style snapshots that omitted recorded provenance

compiled_state.title: Preserve creation provenance through SNAPSHOT compaction

verify_commands.dd_text: Recorded, inferred, and unknown provenance survive retirement; old-style omission recovers from retained CREATE
Legacy and post-feature SNAPSHOT reducer behavior is pinned
Snapshot rollback, consistency, and forward compatibility do not regress
SNAPSHOT output parity, rollback-state documentation, and TicketState output contracts remain synchronized and lint-clean
